Description: Title: Experimental Investigation and Mitigation of Liquid Flow Maldistribution over Vertical Falling Film SurfacesDescription:	In liquid desiccant-based air conditioning systems, uniform liquid distribution over heat and mass transfer surfaces is critical for optimal performance. In the current system, although the liquid is uniformly distributed at the inlet section of a vertical plate, it rapidly converges into streams along the flow path. This maldistribution significantly reduces effective surface wetting, leading to deterioration in heat and mass transfer performance.The objective of this project is to experimentally investigate the flow maldistribution using optical diagnostic techniques and develop strategies to mitigate it. The student will be responsible for commissioning and instrumenting a dedicated test rig, performing controlled experiments across a range of liquid flow rates, and quantitatively characterising the degree of maldistribution. Based on experimental insights, the student will propose, implement, and validate design or operational modifications to improve uniformity and overall system performance.The work will involve systematic experimentation, data acquisition, image processing, analysis, and iterative validation of control techniques.Skill sets:1.      Experimental techniques in thermal and fluid engineering2.      Fundamentals of heat and mass transfer3.      Fluid mechanics and falling film hydrodynamics4.      CAD for rig modifications and distributor redesign5.      Programming (Python) for data analysis and image processing6.      Data acquisition and instrumentation handling7.      Basic optical diagnostics and visualisation techniques8.      Analytical thinking and experimental design methodologyExpected learning:1.      Practical understanding of liquid film hydrodynamics and surface wetting behaviour2.      Hands-on experience in commissioning and operating experimental test rigs3.      Exposure to optical flow visualisation and quantitative image-based analysis4.      Ability to correlate flow distribution with heat and mass transfer performance5.      Experience in iterative engineering design and experimental validation6.      Understanding of how laboratory-scale insights translate into real-world HVAC system performance improvementsSpecific courses required for project execution:1.      Fluid Mechanics2.      Heat Transfer3.      Mass Transfer4.      Engineering Mathematics5.      Basic Programming (preferably Python)6.      Experimental Methods in Mechanical/Chemical Engineering (preferred)  ,Title: Experimental Investigation of Adiabatic Regeneration Performance in Liquid Desiccant Systems under Cross-Flow and Counter-Flow Configurations.Description:	Liquid desiccant systems require continuous regeneration to maintain their moisture absorption capacity and ensure sustained operation in humidity control applications. In adiabatic regeneration mode, the liquid desiccant is directly heated using an external heat source and brought into contact with air to facilitate moisture desorption.The regenerator can be configured in different flow arrangements, primarily cross-flow or counter-flow circuits. Each configuration influences heat and mass transfer characteristics, regeneration effectiveness, thermal efficiency, and overall system performance.	The objective of this project is to evaluate the regeneration performance under different flow configurations experimentally. The student will be involved in commissioning and operating a regeneration test rig, instrumenting the system for temperature, humidity, and flow measurements, and conducting experiments across varying operating conditions (liquid flow rate, air flow rate, inlet temperatures, etc.). The study will compare regeneration efficiency, moisture removal rate, and thermal performance across cross-flow and counter-flow configurations, followed by data-driven analysis and performance benchmarking.Skill sets:1.      Experimental techniques in thermal and fluid engineering2.      Heat and Mass Transfer Fundamentals3.      Thermodynamics of air—water vapour mixtures4.      Psychrometry and humidity measurement techniques5.      Instrumentation and data acquisition systems6.      Programming (Python) for data analysis7.      Basic CFD understanding (preferred but not mandatory)8.      Analytical modelling and performance evaluation9.      Engineering data interpretation and reportingExpected learning:1.      Practical understanding of liquid desiccant regeneration principles2.      Hands-on experience in operating and analysing adiabatic regeneration systems3.      Comparative evaluation of cross-flow vs counter-flow heat and mass exchangers4.      Exposure to psychrometric analysis and performance parameter estimation5.      Development of experimental planning, uncertainty analysis, and validation skills6.      Insight into system-level optimisation of energy-efficient HVAC technologiesSpecific courses required for project execution:1.      Thermodynamics2.      Fluid Mechanics3.      Heat Transfer4.      Mass Transfer5.      Basic HVAC or Refrigeration & Air Conditioning (preferred)6.      Engineering Mathematics7.      Basic Programming (preferably Python)Experimental Methods in Mechanical/Chemical Engineering (preferred)
Title: Experimental Investigation and Mitigation of Liquid Flow Maldistribution over Vertical Falling Film Surfaces,Experimental Investigation of Adiabatic Regeneration Performance in Liquid Desiccant Systems under Cross-Flow and Counter-Flow Configurations.

Description: Title: Experimental Investigation of Pollutant Gas Solubility in Liquid Desiccants and Development of Mitigation Strategies for Performance StabilityDescription:	Liquid desiccant systems operate in direct contact with atmospheric air, making them susceptible to the absorption of ambient gaseous pollutants. Over prolonged operation, dissolution of such pollutants into the desiccant solution may alter its physicochemical properties and degrade system performance.	The solubility of gaseous pollutants in liquid desiccants depends on various parameters, including temperature, gas composition, partial pressure, and the chemical nature of the desiccant solution. This project aims to experimentally evaluate the solubility behaviour of selected ambient pollutant gases in water (as a baseline case) and in different liquid desiccant solutions under controlled variations of temperature and pressure.	Based on the experimental findings, the student will investigate and evaluate potential mitigation strategies to remove or desorb dissolved gaseous pollutants from the desiccant. Techniques to be explored may include physical filtration, adsorption-based filters (e.g., KMn04 media, activated carbon/charcoal, activated alumina), ion exchange resins, temperature-driven desorption, air stripping/injection, and other suitable approaches. The student will be expected to design and develop a laboratory-scale test setup to validate the effectiveness of selected pollutant removal techniques and assess their feasibility for long-term system integration.This project combines experimental chemistry, thermodynamics, mass transfer, and system-level engineering evaluation.Skill sets:1.      Experimental techniques in thermal and chemical engineering2.      Fundamentals of mass transfer and phase equilibrium3.      Basic chemical thermodynamics and gas solubility principles4.      Analytical measurement techniques (gas concentration, solution properties)5.      Design and fabrication of experimental test setups6.      Instrumentation and data acquisition7.      Programming (Python) for data analysis8.      Literature review and comparative technology assessment9.      Critical thinking and engineering evaluation skillsExpected learning:1.      Understanding of gas—liquid equilibrium and solubility behaviour in complex solutions2.      Practical exposure to experimental design for multivariable systems (temperature, pressure, gas type)3.      Insight into long-term operational challenges in liquid desiccant HVAC systems4.      Hands-on experience with adsorption and filtration technologies5.      Ability to compare and evaluate pollutant removal strategies based on technical and practical feasibility6.      Development of system-level thinking for performance stability and lifecycle reliabilitySpecific courses required for project execution:1 . Thermodynamics2.      Mass Transfer3.      Heat Transfer (basic understanding)4.      Fluid Mechanics5.      Physical Chemistry (preferred)2.      Environmental Engineering or Air Pollution Control (preferred)3.      Engineering Mathematics4.      Basic Programming (preferably Python)Experimental Methods in Mechanical/Chemical Engineering (preferred)
Title: Experimental Investigation of Pollutant Gas Solubility in Liquid Desiccants and Development of Mitigation Strategies for Performance Stability

Description: Title: Development and Experimental Validation of Control Logic for Desiccant Exchange and Airflow Modulation under Seasonal Ambient VariationsDescription:Seasonal variations in ambient temperature and humidity result in fluctuating sensible and latent loads on the desiccant systems. To maintain high seasonal efficiency while ensuring desired performance levels, system operation must be dynamically optimised.This project focuses on developing a control system logic that modulates key operational parameters such as cold water flow rate, hot water flow rate, desiccant solution exchange rate, fan RPM, and pump RPM in response to varying ambient conditions. The objective is to optimise seasonal efficiency while maintaining acceptable indoor air conditions and system performance.	The student will develop simulation-based test cases to evaluate different seasonal scenarios and operating strategies. Based on these simulations, a control algorithm will be formulated and implemented. A benchtop-scale experimental rig will then be designed and commissioned to validate the developed control logic under controlled conditions.The project integrates system modelling, control engineering, experimental validation, and performance optimisation.Skill sets:1.      Fundamentals of control systems and feedback control logic2.      Thermodynamics and HVAC system analysis3.      Heat and Mass Transfer4.      System modelling and simulation5.      Programming (Python / MATLAB or equivalent)6.      Basic embedded systems or controller interfacing (preferred)7.      Instrumentation and data acquisition8.      Optimisation techniques9.      Analytical and systems-level thinkingExpected learning:1.      Understanding of seasonal performance optimisation in HVAC systems2.      Hands-on experience in developing and implementing control strategies3.      Exposure to multi-variable system optimisation with performance trade-offs4.      Ability to translate simulation-based insights into experimental validation5.      Practical knowledge of benchtop system integration and controller tuning6.      Insight into energy-efficient system operation and seasonal performance metricsSpecific courses required for project execution:1.      Control Systems2.      Thermodynamics3.      Heat Transfer4.      Mass Transfer5.      Fluid Mechanics6.      HVAC / Refrigeration & Air Conditioning (preferred)7.      Engineering Mathematics8.      Numerical Methods or Simulation Techniques (preferred)Basic Programming (Python / MATLAB preferred)
Title: Development and Experimental Validation of Control Logic for Desiccant Exchange and Airflow Modulation under Seasonal Ambient Variations

The proposed system will function as an intelligent field service assistant, integrating structured checklists with an intuitive UI/UX to guide technicians step-by-step through standard operating procedures. The platform will include features such as dynamic troubleshooting support, automated workflow guidance, digital documentation, and contextual recommendations based on system inputs.The objective is to improve service consistency, reduce human error, enhance technician productivity, and ensure standardised maintenance practices. Description: 1. ObjectiveTo establish a clear relationship between particle size distribution (PSD) and cutting performance ofB4C abrasives (240 and 400 grades) on SiC wafers, and to recommend optimized PSD windows for:• Fast material removal• Controlled surface finishing2. ScopeIncluded:• B4C abrasives (240 grit and 400 mesh)• PSD characterization (laser + sieve)• Controlled lapping trials on SiC platesExcluded:• Any change in machine parameters• Any modification to slurry chemistry• Any sensitive or customer-specific processes3. MaterialsAbrasives• B4C 240 grit – Trial 1 (M) and Trial 2 (F)• B4C 400 mesh – Trial 1 (M) and Trial 2 (F)• One additional blend (to be prepared by team)Substrate• SiC plates (standard size for testing)4. Equipment• Sieve shaker (ASTM sieves: 170, 200, 230, 270, 325, 400, 450, 500, 635)• Laser particle size analyzer• Lapping machine (standard production setup)• Weighing balance (precision ±0.01 g)• Stopwatch• Optical inspection (stereo microscope)5. Methodology5.1 PSD CharacterizationFor each sample:• Measure D10, D50, D90 using laser PSD• Conduct sieve analysis and record % retainedPrepare a comparison table:• Laser PSD vs Sieve distribution5.2 Slurry Preparation• Use standard plant slurry formulation• Maintain constant:o solids %o mixing methodo slurry volumeNo variation allowed.5.3 Lapping TrialsFor each sample:Keep constant:• Machine speed• Load / pressure• Time• Slurry feed ratePerform:• Minimum 3 repeat trials per sample5.4 MeasurementsA. Material Removal Rate (MRR)• Measure weight before and after• Calculate:MRR = (Weight loss) / (Time)B. Surface Evaluation• Visual inspection under microscope• Record:o scratch depth (qualitative)o uniformityo presence of deep groovesC. Slurry BehaviourObserve and record:• settling rate• flow behaviour• “cutting feel” (gritty vs smooth)6. Experimental Matrix—exampleSample Type DescriptionS1 240-M Medium distributionS2 240-F Fast cut distributionS3 400-M Medium distributionS4 400-F Fast cut distributionS5 Custom Blend (student designed)7. Data AnalysisPrepare:7.1 PSD vs Performance• D50 vs MRR• D90 vs cutting aggressiveness7.2 Sieve Fraction vs Performance• % coarse fraction vs MRR• % fines vs surface finish8. Expected OutcomesStudents must clearly establish:1. Effect of coarse particles on cutting speed2. Effect of fines on surface finish3. Optimum balance for:o Fast cuttingo Controlled finishing9. Final Deliverables1. PSD Recommendation TablesFor both 240 and 400 grades:• Sieve distribution• D10 / D50 / D90 ranges2. Performance Charts• PSD vs MRR• PSD vs surface quality3. Practical Recommendations• Ideal PSD for SiC wafer cutting• Do’s and Don’ts for abrasive preparation4. Final Report• Maximum 10–12 pages• Data-driven (no theory-heavy content)• Clear conclusions10. Constraints• No deviation from defined test conditions• No access to restricted production areas• All trials to be supervised11. Evaluation CriteriaStudents will be evaluated based on:• Accuracy of data• Repeatability of results• Clarity of conclusions• Practical usefulness of recommendations12. Key Guidance• “More data, less theory”• “If results are not repeatable, they are not valid”• “Focus on what improves cutting — not what looks good on paper”Final NoteThis project is expected to directly support ongoing development work and generate usablespecifications for industrial application.
Title: Optimization of Boron Carbide (B4C) Abrasive PSD for SiC Wafer Lapping Applications

Description: Description: The aim of this project is to analyze video recordings of insects navigating an open arena under various experimental conditions. This requires training a deep neural network model to identify specific types of insects in images and segment them (instance segmentation). The candidate will plug the trained model into the Argos toolkit and use it to extract the tracks of the insects over time. They will then analyze these tracks to identify the strategies that that insects use for navigation. They will also use pose estimation tools like DeepLabCut to find finer details of the posture of the animals and classify their behavior. Finally, they will standardize and document these methods to develop a workflow for analyzing animal behavior from video recordings.Skill sets: Python programming, Scientific Python (scipy, scikits-learn, Pytorch), AI/ML, Image processingExpected learning: Software engineering, AI/ML, Computer VisionSpecific courses required for project execution: Computer Programming, Introductory AI/ML, Image Processing, Computer Vision
Title: Title: Video analysis for animal behavior

Description: Description: Coal and Lignite are important fossil fuels, used for electric power generation, besides use in the boiler of industries. They are the most demanded energy sources, and their consumption has been increasing. However, there is a need to improve coal combustion performance to enhance the efficiency of furnace and boiler. Addition of additives to both fuels is an option and has led to focus by researchers on developing additive. Such additives can effectively improve the combustion performance of the coal and lignite. A study on the subject would contribute to providing information about the impact of additives on combustion properties, calorific value and a hybrid technology that may use additives to modify the coal/lignite surface. Thus, the objective of this study is to relate the effect of additives on utilization and combustion performances of both the fuels.Improved efficiency and reduced emissions from coal/lignite power stations may lead to development of present and future efficient coal/lignite combustion technologies. Upgrading coal, reducing emission of NOx, SO2 and particulate matter, and corrosion are some of the factors in the development of efficient coal technologies. Such additive could be a finely milled 2 multi-oxide/carbonate additives when mixed with the coal/lignite without the need to change the boiler installation has proven to increase the boiler efficiency, flame temperature with reduction in corrosion, NOx and particulate matter (dust) emissions. The process may be developed through bench, pilot and full scale. It would be tested at lab scale and actual operation.Skill sets: Instrumentation / Chemical AnalysisExpected learning: Understanding of Combustion Properties, Additive Development and applicationSpecific courses required for project execution: Chemical Engineering / Materials Science & Engineering / Chemistry
Title: Title: Development of Coal and Lignite additives to improve their combustions, efficiency, calorific value, and reduction in pollution levels.

Description: Description: The traditional approach in carrying out mineral exploration studies included collection of samples including rock drills cores and analyzing them in the laboratory to generate data. This used to be costly, time consuming, and laborious for mine areas with difficult terrain. With the advent of latest technologies, especially field portable analytical instruments viz. portable visible and near-infrared spectrophotometers, gamma-ray spectrometer, LED fluorimeter, pXRF, pXRD, pLIBS, and muRaman spectrometer, their on-site use has increased in mineral exploration. Furthermore, use of these instruments on site do help in quick, real-time, non-destructive, and cost-effective identification and determination of indicator minerals and pathfinder elements in rock and ores, in soil sediment, and in water samples. Such portable analytical instruments determine accurate chemical and mineralogical data during the field visits with virtually no or minimum laboratory analysis, thus bypassing the need for sample collection. The information so obtained helps in decision making in carrying out field work as also during drilling operations in mineral exploration.In our research use of such portable devices would be made in carrying out exploration studies for Gold (Au), Silver (Ag), Rubidium (Rb), Hafnium (Hf), Zirconium (Zr), and a group of 17 Rare Earth Elements (REE) in Ambaji mines.Skill sets: Instrumentation / Chemical AnalysisExpected learning: a. It would help identify the indicator minerals and path finder elements, making the mining activity more focused. b. It would promote sustainable mining including underground mining. c. It would promote collaboration with the technology providers across the world. d. It would lead to exchange of experts on the issue from Universities and Institutes of repute across the world, including collaboration with such organizations.
Title: Title: Identifying Indicator minerals and Pathfinder elements in mineral exploration.

Description: Description: This project aims to develop and implement an Inverse Synthetic Aperture Radar (ISAR)imaging system using MATLAB or Python, focusing on signal processing algorithms and imageformation techniques. ISAR is a powerful radar imaging methodology used to generate high-resolutionimages of moving targets, such as aircraft or ships, by exploiting their relative motion with respect to theradar platform.This work has practical relevance in defense surveillance, remote sensing, target identification, andreconnaissance operations. It also provides hands-on exposure to core concepts in digital signalprocessing, time-frequency analysis, and radar system design.Skill sets:• Strong understanding of radar principles and wave propagation• Proficiency in MATLAB and/or Python for scientific computing• Knowledge of signal processing techniques (e.g., FFT, filtering, windowing)• Familiarity with radar imaging concepts (e.g., range-Doppler, cross-range resolution)• Experience with data visualization and image processing libraries (e.g., matplotlib, OpenCV)• Ability to work with simulated or real radar datasets• Problem-solving and debugging skills in a programming environment• Technical writing and documentation proficiencyExpected learning:• Comprehensive understanding of ISAR image formation theory and techniques• Hands-on experience with radar signal simulation and processing in MATLAB/Python• Ability to implement and optimize algorithms for motion compensation and focusing• Familiarity with real-world radar challenges like noise, clutter, and resolution limits• Insight into advanced signal processing methods used in modern radar systems• Development of software and analytical skills critical to defense and aerospace R&DSpecific courses required for project execution:• Digital Signal Processing – for handling and transforming radar signals• Electromagnetic Wave Theory – for understanding wave interactions and radar returns• Programming for Engineers – for implementing and debugging ISAR algorithms• Pattern Recognition or Image Processing – for target interpretation and analysis• Time-Frequency Analysis – for dynamic target signal characterization• Applied Linear Algebra – for signal transformation and filtering techniques
Title: Title: Image processing using MATLAB/Python

Description: Description: This project is focused on the end-to-end design, simulation, and performance evaluationof a wideband standard horn antenna operating across the 800 MHz to 18 GHz frequency range. Suchultra-wideband (UWB) horn antennas are critical components in broadband measurement systems,EMC testing, radar front ends, and antenna calibration setups.The primary objective is to create a horn antenna with high gain, low VSWR, and stable radiationpatterns across the entire band. The project will explore various horn geometries (pyramidal, conical,and dual-ridged), waveguide transitions, impedance matching techniques, and material considerationsto achieve wideband operation..Skill sets:• Understanding of antenna theory and electromagnetic wave propagation• Proficiency in 3D electromagnetic simulation (e.g., HFSS, CST, FEKO)• Basic knowledge of waveguide theory and transition design• Mechanical design using CAD tools (e.g., SolidWorks, Fusion 360)• Scripting and automation for parametric sweeps (e.g., MATLAB, Python)• Familiarity with RF measurement equipment (e.g., VNA, spectrum analyzer)• Technical documentation and research reportingExpected learning:• Ability to design and optimize horn antennas over wide frequency ranges• Deep understanding of waveguide feeding structures and impedance matching• Proficiency in simulation of complex EM structures and interpretation of S-parameters• Hands-on knowledge of CAD modeling and preparing fabrication-ready designs• Insights into real-world fabrication constraints and RF testing methodologies• Enhanced analytical thinking and multi-domain design capability (EM + mechanical)Specific courses required for project execution:• Antenna Theory and Design – for radiation mechanism and parameter tuning• Microwave Engineering – for transmission line and waveguide concepts• Electromagnetic Field Theory – for understanding wave propagation and boundary conditions• Computer-Aided Design (CAD) – for mechanical modeling of antenna structures• High-Frequency Simulation Techniques – using tools like CST, HFSS, COMSOL• RF & Microwave Measurements – for post-fabrication validation• Numerical Techniques in Electromagnetics – for solving Maxwell’s equations in complexgeometries
Title: Design and Optimization of a Wideband Standard Horn Antenna (800 MHz – 18 GHz)

Description: Description: Quantum optics experiments at QuIC Lab require tight real-time control overmultiple physical parameters simultaneously — laser power, component temperatures, andoptomechanical positioning. Drifts in any of these degrade experimental fidelity. Theengineering challenge is to implement reliable, tunable closed-loop PID controllers onArduino and Raspberry Pi — each suited to different control bandwidths — and integratethem into active lab pipelines.The intern will focus on one or two control subsystems within the 2-month window. Theywill understand the control requirements of the assigned subsystem, implement PID logicin embedded C/C++ for Arduino and Python for Raspberry Pi, tune the controller againstthe real plant response, and deliver a tested, integrated module. The codebase must bestructured so it can be adapted to other subsystems by the lab without assistance. This isnot a simulation exercise — the code will run on live equipment. The intern is expected toread datasheets, debug hardware independently, and document their work thoroughly.Required Skills & Traits Embedded C / C++ | Python | Arduino IDE | Raspberry PiGPIO | Control systems — PID theory | ADC/DAC interfacing | Hardware debugging |Datasheet reading | Clean code & documentationExpected Outcomes: 1. Tuned, tested PID controller module integrated into at least oneactive experimental pipeline, with a second subsystem completed if time permits.2. Documented codebase structured for straightforward adaptation to additional controlsubsystems by the lab.3. Technical report covering controller design, tuning methodology, measured stepresponse per platform, and known limitations.Specific courses required: Control Systems; Microprocessors & Microcontrollers; Signals& SystemsLab coursework with hands-on hardware experience is strongly preferred over theory-onlyexposure.
Title: Title: Embedded PID Controller Design and Integration for Laser, Temperature, and  Optomechanical Control using Arduino and Raspberry Pi

Description: Description: A multi-agent AI system orchestrates engineering change management workflowsacross Teamcenter. The orchestrator, its agents, and the workflow framework are already built andoperational. However, the system currently has no connectivity to data outside Teamcenter.Engineering decisions routinely depend on information distributed across Teamcenter, SharePoint,and the broader M365 ecosystem. The orchestrator cannot access, correlate, or present this cross-system information today.Skill sets: C#, Basics of Object-Oriented programming, MCP, AgentsExpected learning:Project ScopeBuild the agents, MCP servers, tools, connectors, and data source queries needed to give the existingorchestrator access to Teamcenter and M365 / SharePoint data. The work includes designing andimplementing the agents that reason over cross-system information, the tool servers that exposequery capabilities, the connection and query logic against each data source&#39;s APIs, and anormalization layer that makes structurally different source data semantically consistent for theorchestrator&#39;s agents. All outputs must support multiple rendering formats — text, tables, lists, cards,document previews, relationship graphs, and timelines — so the orchestrator can present resultsappropriately.The components must support both independent invocation against a single data source andcomposed invocation across both sources in a single orchestrated flow.Deliverables1. Document and Draw the features covering agents, tools, data flows, API mappings, and schemadefinitions2. Teamcenter agents, tools, and connectors with at least 20 operational query capabilities3. M365 / SharePoint agents, tools, and connectors with at least 5 operational query capabilities4. Multi-view output system supporting at least 4 distinct rendering formats5. Live integration demonstration6. Test suite covering unit and integration scenariosSpecific courses required for project execution: Object Oriented Programming
Title: Enterprise Data Connectors for AI-Driven Engineering Workflows

Description: Description:This project focuses on building a personalized, multimodal agentic AI system capable of resolving student doubts through voice, text, and image inputs. The AI agent contextually understands questions across subjects and responds with step-by-step explanations in a conversational, human-like manner. The system integrates with WhatsApp Business API to enable autonomous doubt resolution directly on the platform students already use. Additionally, a native Android application is developed to provide a seamless mobile experience. The project emphasizes real-time inference, multimodal understanding, and autonomous conversation management without human escalation for standard queries.Skill sets:Multimodal AI & Speech Processing: Handling text, voice, and image-based doubt inputs using LLMs and vision modelsAgentic Conversation Design: Building autonomous, context-aware dialogue agents that maintain session historyWhatsApp Business API Integration: Enabling automated doubt solving through WhatsApp using the Meta Cloud APIAndroid App Development: Building a mobile application for doubt submission, history, and AI interactionPersonalization & Adaptive Response: Tailoring explanations to the user's grade level, learning pace, and prior contextExpected learning:Gaining hands-on experience in multimodal AI system design combining voice, text, and image understandingImplementing WhatsApp API-based autonomous workflows for real-world student interactionDeveloping a production-grade Android application integrated with an AI backendDesigning personalized conversational agents that adapt to individual student needsUnderstanding real-time inference pipelines for low-latency doubt resolutionSpecific courses required for project execution:Natural Language Processing & Conversational AI (for dialogue management and response generation)Computer Vision & Multimodal Learning (for image-based doubt interpretation)Mobile Application Development – Android (for building the student-facing app)Software Engineering & API Integration (for WhatsApp Business API and backend orchestration)
Title: Title: Doubt Solver – Multimodal Talking Agentic AI with WhatsApp Integration & Android App

Description: Description:This project involves building an agentic AI system that functions as a Subject Matter Expert (SME) in K12 mathematics, capable of autonomously generating high-quality, curriculum-aligned practice content at scale. The system is designed to produce over 10,000 pages of structured mathematics practice material spanning Grades 1 through 12, covering topics from foundational arithmetic to advanced algebra, geometry, and calculus. The generated content is deployed to a production environment accessible to students and educators. A web-based admin panel is developed for content review, quality control, topic configuration, and deployment management. The project emphasizes prompt engineering, content quality assurance pipelines, and scalable AI deployment.Skill sets:Agentic AI & Prompt Engineering: Designing LLM-based agents with structured prompting for consistent, accurate mathematical content generationCurriculum Design & K12 Mathematics: Understanding grade-wise learning objectives and structuring content accordinglyContent Quality Assurance & Evaluation: Implementing automated and human-in-the-loop pipelines to validate mathematical accuracyProduction Deployment & DevOps: Deploying AI-generated content systems to scalable cloud infrastructureWeb Development (Admin Panel): Building a full-stack interface for content management, review workflows, and deployment controlsExpected learning:Gaining hands-on experience in large-scale AI content generation using agentic workflowsImplementing K12 curriculum-aligned mathematics content pipelines from generation to deploymentDeveloping content quality control systems combining automated validation and structured reviewBuilding admin-facing web tools for managing, filtering, and deploying AI-generated educational materialSpecific courses required for project execution:Natural Language Processing & Large Language Models (for content generation and prompt engineering)Mathematics & Curriculum Studies (for K12-aligned content structuring and accuracy)Web Technologies & Full-Stack Development (for admin panel and content management interface)
Title: Title: Academic SME Agentic AI – K12 Mathematics Content Generation & Deployment at Scale

Description: Description:The primary goal of this project is to automate the purity analysis of agricultural seeds. Inthe seed industry, distinguishing between the target seed and Other DistinguishableVarieties (ODV)—seeds that look similar but belong to different varieties—is a critical butlabor-intensive manual task.PS team will develop a vision-based system that uses high-resolution imaging to captureseed samples and apply Deep Learning algorithms to classify them. The system will detectphysical defects, size variations, and subtle morphological differences that define ODVs,ensuring that seed batches meet strict certification standards.Skill Sets:Programming: Proficiency in Python (specifically libraries like NumPy, Pandas, andMatplotlib).Computer Vision: Experience with OpenCV for image preprocessing, filtering, andsegmentation.Machine Learning/Deep Learning: Familiarity with frameworks such as PyTorch orTensorFlow/Keras.Data Handling: Knowledge of data annotation tools (e.g., CVAT, LabelImg, orRoboflow) and dataset balancing techniques.Expected Learning:End-to-End AI Lifecycle: You will gain hands-on experience in the full pipeline—from raw image acquisition and data labeling to model training and evaluation.Model Architecture: Understanding and implementing state-of-the-artarchitectures, such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) or VisionTransformers (ViTs), specifically optimized for small-object detection.Performance Metrics: Learning how to evaluate model success beyond justaccuracy, using Precision-Recall curves, F1-scores, and Confusion Matrices toaccount for the high stakes of seed purity.Optimization: Techniques like transfer learning and hyperparameter tuning toimprove model robustness against varying lighting and seed orientations.Specific Courses Required for Project Execution:To successfully execute this project, foundational knowledge in the following areas ishighly recommended:1. Digital Image Processing (DIP): To understand how to clean and enhance imagedata before it hits the model.2. Machine Learning / Deep Learning: Core theory on neural networks,backpropagation, and loss functions.3. Linear Algebra & Probability: Essential for understanding high-dimensional datatransformations and statistical confidence in predictions.4. Object Detection and Recognition: Specialized coursework or modules focusingon spatial localization within images.,This project focuses on building a "Track and Trace" ecosystem for the agricultural sector.You will be working on the digital backbone that ensures every single seed pouch can beaccounted for from the packing line to the end consumer (Farmer), utilizing high-speedvision systems and hierarchical data structures.Title: TraceabilityDescription: The project involves implementing an end-to-end traceability system on ahigh-speed seed packing line. The core objective is to establish a Parent-Childrelationship across three layers of packaging:1. Primary: Individual seed pouches with unique QR codes.2. Secondary: Bundles or boxes containing a specific set of pouches.3. Tertiary (Shipper): Master cartons containing multiple secondary packs.You will work with Vision Inspection Systems that use AI/ML models to detect anddecode multiple QR codes simultaneously (aggregation), even under challengingconditions like overlapping pouches or high-speed motion. The system validates eachscan against a central database to ensure no duplicates or mismatches occur beforecommissioning the batch for shipment.Skill SetsPython Programming: Proficiency in Python for backend logic, data handling, andintegration with vision libraries.Computer Vision: Experience with OpenCV or similar frameworks for image preprocessingand QR detection.AI/ML Frameworks: Basic understanding of TensorFlow or PyTorch for pouchdetection and segmentation during the aggregation process.HW & NW Integration: * Interfacing with industrial hardware (C-mount cameras,triggers, and PLCs).Network protocols (TCP/IP, MQTT, or HTTP) for communication between theedge camera and the central server.Database Management: Basic SQL (PostgreSQL/MySQL) to manage thehierarchical Parent-Child data tables.Expected LearningIndustrial IoT (IIoT) Workflow: Understand how software interacts with physicalmachinery in a real-time manufacturing environment.Data Aggregation Logic: Learn the complexity of "Parent-Child" mapping where theChild IDs are logically bound to a "Parent" ID in real-time.Edge AI Deployment: How to deploy ML models on edge devices to perform highspeedinference without latency.System Robustness: Developing "Validation Logic" to handle exceptions, such asunreadable codes or missing items in a sequence.Supply Chain Standards: Familiarity with global standards for traceability (likeGS1) often used in logistics.Specific Courses Required for Project ExecutionDigital Image Processing (DIP): Essential for understanding how to manipulateand clean images for better QR recognition.Machine Learning / Deep Learning: To understand the models used for objectdetection (detecting pouches on a conveyor).Industrial Automation / Robotics: To understand the hardware interface and thesynchronization between the conveyor belt and the camera trigger.Database Management Systems (DBMS): To design and query the relationalstructures required for multi-level packaging.Computer Networks: To manage the data flow between the packing line, the localcontroller, and the cloud/enterprise server.
Title: Vision Inspection: AI-Powered Seed Quality & ODV Identification,Seed Traceability & Aggregation
